title: LifeOS direct database control surface defined
goal: Define the LifeOS Mission Control path that reads, directs, and advances the canonical PostgreSQL state through local UDS and redb-backed low-latency views without HTTP or flat-file control-plane detours.
verification_gate: Every visible LifeOS state and command maps to canonical database identities, explicit authority, UDS request and response contracts, freshness semantics, audit events, rollback, and no hidden filesystem write path.
supporting_sources: 1.0_VISION/Notebooklm/Architecture Context Matrix_ PostgreSQL-First LifeOS Framework - Table 1.csv

source_anchors.lifeos_is_mission_control: Architecture Context Matrix row 1 (RuVector/Postgres): 'LifeOS provides the Mission Control UI to the Postgres-hosted codebase.'
source_anchors.uds_not_http: Blueprint 'LifeOS Unix Domain Sockets (UDS) via Yazelix (Zellij)': 'LifeOS avoids using standard HTTP/REST ... Instead, it connects directly via Unix Domain Sockets (UDS) or by reading the shared redb state files.'
source_anchors.lifeos_reads_redb_via_uds: Architecture Context Matrix row 3 (redb): 'LifeOS reads redb files via UDS for real-time agent status.'
source_anchors.envctl_uds_to_lifeos: Architecture Context Matrix row 10 (envctl + nu_plugin): 'Communicates with LifeOS via UDS for UI rendering.'
source_anchors.bun_ffi_mmap: Blueprint 7. 'Bun/Bunx: ... FFI provides zero-network overhead, allowing the UI to read the same memory-mapped redb files used by the agents.'
source_anchors.msgpack_no_json: Blueprint 5. 'nu_plugin_ruvector: Streams tables into memory using a native MessagePack-based protocol to avoid JSON serialization bottlenecks.'
source_anchors.semantic_edit_sql_update: Blueprint: 'agents use standard SQL combined with RuVector hybrid search (ORDER BY semantic_embedding <->) ... uses standard SQL UPDATE statements to rewrite the code directly within those specific database rows.'
source_anchors.cow_branching: Blueprint 5/2: 'clones the current codebase state into isolated staging branches ... Once automated tests confirm a branch is mathematically stable ... merged into the main trunk. If a branch fails, the database simply rolls it back.'
source_anchors.mincut: Blueprint 6: 'RuVector MinCut ... severs that agent's connection in milliseconds, isolating the failure.'
source_anchors.witness_chains: Blueprint 6: 'Cryptographic Witness Chains (SHAKE256): Every code snippet or decision is linked to a cryptographic audit trail originating from a verified source vector.'
source_anchors.envctl_projection: Blueprint: 'envctl queries PostgreSQL for the final production branch ... uses module_path metadata ... concatenates raw_code blocks ... dumps this clean directory into your Nix or Yazelix build environment.'
source_anchors.ruflo_human_in_loop: Architecture Context Matrix row 6/16 (Ruflo): 'Interfaces with LifeOS for human-in-the-loop control.'
source_anchors.atas_timelines: Blueprint 4: 'Future Timelines consisting of: Projected Git Diffs ... Resource Burn ... Complexity Spikes ... Failure Nodes.'
source_anchors.latency_tiers: Blueprint 1 table: redb 'Microseconds (In-process)' / PostgreSQL+RuVector 'Milliseconds (Network/IPC)'; Persistence 'Process Life-cycle / Local Disk' vs 'Enterprise ACID / PITR Recovery'.
source_anchors.pitr_recovery: Blueprint 1 table: PostgreSQL+RuVector persistence = 'Enterprise ACID / PITR Recovery'.

honored_invariants: Single durable writer: PostgreSQL owns every durable macro-state class exactly once; LifeOS Mission Control is a reader and a command issuer, never a second durable writer.

Forward-only sync: LifeOS-issued directives that touch high-frequency state land in redb and are flushed redb -> envctl -> PostgreSQL; LifeOS never establishes an undirected redb<->PostgreSQL dual-write.
redb is the transient high-frequency VIEW tier, not a control-plane authority: LifeOS memory-maps redb read-only for microsecond live status; canonical settled truth is re-read from PostgreSQL.
No sidecar: every semantic/vector read the surface renders is served by RuVector as a native PostgreSQL extension via the same UDS path, not a separate vector service.
Provenance preserved: every command's audit event carries the SHAKE256 witness chain rooted in a PostgreSQL source vector.

no_http_rest.rationale: Blueprint explicitly forbids HTTP/REST between LifeOS and the runtime; the surface exposes no HTTP listener and issues no HTTP request.

no_flat_file_control_plane.rationale: No directive is spooled to, and no visible state is sourced from, a flat control-plane file. Commands travel over the control UDS to the canonical database; live views are memory-mapped redb reads (a transient view, not a control-plane file).

control_uds.server: envctl runtime bridge (single canonical writer path into PostgreSQL)
control_uds.encoding: MessagePack request/response frames (never JSON text on the wire)
control_uds.framing: length-prefixed MessagePack; each request carries {method, params, request_id}; each response carries {request_id, result | error, audit_ref}
control_uds.method_registry: the union of every visible_states[].uds_contract.method and command_surfaces[].uds_contract.method defined below

shared_state_channel.access: LifeOS opens the same redb file the agents write, mmap read-only; agents are the sole writers, LifeOS never writes this file
shared_state_channel.purpose: microsecond-latency live views (swarm status, ingestion buffer depth) that would bottleneck if round-tripped to PostgreSQL
shared_state_channel.settled_truth: significant states are flushed forward by envctl and re-read canonically from PostgreSQL; the redb view is never treated as durable authority

tiers.meaning: In-process microsecond view read via mmap; unsettled, may be superseded or rolled back; last-writer-wins per key.
tiers.authority: non-authoritative view of transient high-frequency state owned by redb per STORE-001

tiers.meaning: ACID-consistent snapshot served over the control UDS; the settled, durable truth.
tiers.staleness_bound: milliseconds (IPC/native-extension call), read-your-writes after a committed command

tiers.meaning: Ensemble simulations, MinCut graph states, routing decisions: computed on demand, non-authoritative, discarded unless a COW branch is committed.
tiers.staleness_bound: per-simulation-cycle; never persisted as truth until committed to the main trunk
tiers.authority: no durable owner until commit; ATAS/MinCut/RuvLTRA runtime

filesystem_write_paths.assertion: Every filesystem write reachable from the LifeOS Mission Control surface is enumerated here; none is a hidden control-plane detour.

enumerated.classification: derived + disposable; deterministically re-projectable from PostgreSQL rows via module_path metadata; never a source of truth

enumerated.trigger: written by agents, NOT by LifeOS; LifeOS access is read-only mmap
enumerated.classification: transient high-frequency tier per STORE-001; process-lifecycle; forward-flushed to PostgreSQL; not a LifeOS write path

enumerated.path_class: current LifeOS local UI view-state (transitional Tauri persistence whitelist)
enumerated.trigger: debounced UI ergonomics writes in the current product (src/lib/persistence.ts LIFEOS_PERSIST_KEYS)
enumerated.classification: non-canonical, disposable UI ergonomics only; explicitly whitelisted (never domain truth); in the intended architecture domain state moves to PostgreSQL-via-UDS while this local cache stays a disposable view
enumerated.source: verified in src/lib/persistence.ts + LifeOS CLAUDE.md persistence contract

visible_states.what_david_sees: Real-time agent thoughts, terminal traces, heartbeats, and per-agent state flags of the running swarm.
visible_states.canonical_identity: agent_id + thought/trace keys; transient in redb, forward-flushed to PostgreSQL as settled cognitive states (significant-state rows keyed by agent_id + cycle_id)
visible_states.authority: redb (per node) for the live view; PostgreSQL for settled/significant states flushed by envctl (STORE-001 transient-class)
visible_states.freshness: redb-live (microseconds) for the running view; postgres-canonical for settled history
visible_states.audit_event: read-provenance: each snapshot response carries source = {redb-mmap | postgres} and last_flush_cycle_id so the operator sees settled-vs-unsettled lineage
visible_states.rollback: non-mutating read; nothing to roll back; unsettled entries are superseded by the next canonical flush (canonical re-read reconciles)

visible_states.what_david_sees: Functions, structs, AST fragments, and code rows of the OS-as-database with module_path and status.
visible_states.canonical_identity: codebase_table rows: (row_id, module_path, raw_code, language, status, semantic_embedding, causal_dependency_edges)
visible_states.authority: PostgreSQL + RuVector (single durable owner of durable relational macro-state, STORE-001)
visible_states.audit_event: read-provenance: query returns branch_id + snapshot_lsn so every rendered row is traceable to a PostgreSQL point-in-time
visible_states.rollback: non-mutating read; PITR-consistent snapshot; no local write to reverse

visible_states.what_david_sees: Top-k semantically relevant functions for a query, ranked by vector distance combined with relational filters.
visible_states.canonical_identity: codebase_table rows ordered by (semantic_embedding <-> :target_vector) under relational predicates
visible_states.authority: PostgreSQL + RuVector native extension (No-Sidecar; STORE-001 semantic-class)
visible_states.freshness: postgres-canonical (milliseconds); embeddings from all-MiniLM-L6-v2 committed during envctl bridge
visible_states.audit_event: read-provenance: each match carries source_vector_id enabling witness-chain lineage back to ingestion
visible_states.rollback: non-mutating read; nothing to roll back

visible_states.what_david_sees: The main trunk plus every isolated staging branch, its parent, agent owner, and merge/rollback state.
visible_states.canonical_identity: cow_branch rows: (branch_id, parent_branch_id, agent_state_id, esn_score, status[staging|merged|discarded])
visible_states.authority: PostgreSQL (COW branches live inside the database; STORE-001 durable-class)
visible_states.audit_event: read-provenance: branch rows carry created_by_command_id linking to the CS-* audit event that opened them

visible_states.what_david_sees: Projected git diffs, resource burn, complexity spikes, and failure nodes for simulated branches up to 100 steps ahead.
visible_states.canonical_identity: non-authoritative simulation rows keyed by (sim_id, branch_id, step): {state_diff, resource_burn, complexity_spike, failure_node}
visible_states.authority: ATAS runtime (transient-derived); no durable owner until a branch is committed to the trunk
visible_states.audit_event: read-provenance: response marks authority=transient-derived and sim_id so the operator never mistakes a forecast for committed truth
visible_states.rollback: non-mutating read; simulations are discarded unless CS-02 commits the winning branch

visible_states.what_david_sees: The live dependency graph with weakest-link cuts and any agent nodes MinCut has severed.
visible_states.canonical_identity: dependency graph over PostgreSQL dependency tables + transient severed-node set (agent_id, cut_weight)
visible_states.authority: RuVector MinCut runtime over PostgreSQL dependency tables (graph is canonical; severed-set is transient-derived)
visible_states.freshness: transient-derived for cuts (subpolynomial live updates); postgres-canonical for the underlying dependency edges
visible_states.audit_event: read-provenance: each severed entry carries the causal-edge id that failed verification
visible_states.rollback: non-mutating read; a reroute is reversible at CS-05 (graph state, not a stored mutation)

visible_states.what_david_sees: The SHAKE256 provenance chain binding each code edit / agent decision to its verified source vector.
visible_states.canonical_identity: witness_chain rows: (event_id, shake256_root, source_vector_id, subject_row_id, actor)
visible_states.authority: PostgreSQL (source vectors + chains) with AgentDB-rooted agent decisions (STORE-001 durable + per-agent classes)
visible_states.audit_event: self-describing: this surface IS the audit trail; reads are logged with the requesting actor
visible_states.rollback: non-mutating read; chains are append-only and never rewritten

visible_states.what_david_sees: RuvLTRA proportional-intelligence routing: task complexity, chosen model tier (local ruvllm vs Claude Opus/GPT-4), cost/complexity metrics.
visible_states.canonical_identity: routing_policy rows (policy_id, tier_rules) in PostgreSQL meta-analytics + transient per-task routing decisions (task_id, model_id, complexity_score)
visible_states.authority: Ruflo / RuvLTRA (routing policies are canonical in PostgreSQL; live decisions are transient-derived); Ruflo interfaces with LifeOS for human-in-the-loop control
visible_states.freshness: postgres-canonical for policies; transient-derived for live decisions
visible_states.audit_event: read-provenance: decisions carry policy_id + resolved_at so routing is explainable
visible_states.rollback: non-mutating read; policy changes are governed by CS-04-adjacent policy versioning

visible_states.what_david_sees: redb buffer depth, BLAKE3 dedup cache hit rate, and envctl bridge progress (blocks pulled / embedded / committed to PostgreSQL).
visible_states.canonical_identity: redb buffer counters (transient) + PostgreSQL commit counters (ingested_blocks_committed, dedup_cache_hits)
visible_states.authority: nu_plugin/redb for buffer view; PostgreSQL for committed counts; envctl is the forward bridge (STORE-001 forward-only sync)
visible_states.freshness: redb-live (microseconds) for buffer depth; postgres-canonical for committed totals
visible_states.audit_event: read-provenance: committed counts tie to envctl flush_cycle_id
visible_states.rollback: non-mutating read; buffered-not-committed blocks are transient and discardable

visible_states.what_david_sees: The current production branch, its module_path directory map, and the last envctl projection result (target build env, byte-parity status).
visible_states.canonical_identity: PostgreSQL production branch (branch_id, is_production) + module_path metadata rows; the projected directory is derived output
visible_states.authority: PostgreSQL for the branch + metadata; envctl projection engine (read-only from PostgreSQL) for the derived tree (STORE-001 derived-projection class)
visible_states.freshness: postgres-canonical for branch/metadata; derived output reflects the last CS-07 run
visible_states.audit_event: read-provenance: last_projection carries the CS-07 command_id and SHA identity of the projected tree
visible_states.rollback: non-mutating read; the derived tree is re-projectable
visible_states.filesystem_write_path: reads only; the projected tree itself is written solely by CS-07 (enumerated, derived, disposable)

command_surfaces.what_david_directs: Find a function by meaning and rewrite its row inside an isolated COW branch.
command_surfaces.canonical_identity: target codebase_table.row_id resolved via semantic_embedding <-> :target_vector; edit applied as SQL UPDATE within cow_branch.branch_id
command_surfaces.authority: PostgreSQL is the single durable writer; the edit is issued over the control UDS and applied by the agent/envctl writer path (STORE-001 single-writer invariant) — LifeOS never writes PostgreSQL directly
command_surfaces.freshness: ack immediate over UDS; effect is postgres-canonical and read-your-writes on branch_id after commit of the UPDATE
command_surfaces.audit_event: SHAKE256 witness-chain event bound to the source_vector_id of the target row; rejected if no causal edge / historical root exists (Causal Graph Verification)
command_surfaces.rollback: discard the COW branch (CS-03) before merge; after merge, PostgreSQL PITR restores the prior trunk state
command_surfaces.filesystem_write_path: none (writes are database rows, not files)

command_surfaces.what_david_directs: Promote a verified staging branch into the main trunk, advancing canonical state.
command_surfaces.canonical_identity: cow_branch.branch_id (staging) merged into is_production trunk; produces new trunk revision
command_surfaces.authority: PostgreSQL (COW merge inside the database); gated by MinCut integrity + ESN stability before merge (STORE-001 durable-class)
command_surfaces.freshness: postgres-canonical; trunk_revision becomes the new read-your-writes baseline for RS-02/RS-04/RS-10
command_surfaces.audit_event: SHAKE256 witness event linking the merged rows to their source vectors; merge refused if any row lacks a causal edge
command_surfaces.rollback: PostgreSQL PITR to the pre-merge revision; the discarded staging branch remains recoverable until GC

command_surfaces.what_david_directs: Abandon a failing staging branch so its edits never touch the trunk.
command_surfaces.authority: PostgreSQL (branch rollback is a native database operation)
command_surfaces.freshness: postgres-canonical; branch disappears from RS-04 immediately after commit of the status change
command_surfaces.audit_event: SHAKE256 witness event recording the discard reason and the actor
command_surfaces.rollback: the discard is itself the rollback; a mistakenly discarded branch is recoverable from PostgreSQL PITR until GC

command_surfaces.what_david_directs: Direct the swarm: assign a task and let RuvLTRA route it to the correct model tier, human-in-the-loop.
command_surfaces.canonical_identity: task row (task_id, intent, target_scope) + routing decision (model_id, tier) recorded in PostgreSQL meta-analytics; live queue buffered in redb
command_surfaces.authority: Ruflo / RuvLTRA orchestrator; canonical task + policy in PostgreSQL, transient dispatch buffered in redb then flushed forward (STORE-001 forward-only)
command_surfaces.freshness: ack immediate; canonical task row postgres-canonical after flush; live progress visible via RS-01/RS-08
command_surfaces.audit_event: SHAKE256 witness event binding the dispatch to the requesting actor and resolved policy_id
command_surfaces.rollback: cancel the task (idempotent stop); no durable trunk mutation occurs from dispatch alone; buffered dispatch is transient

command_surfaces.what_david_directs: Manually trigger MinCut to isolate a hallucinating or drifting agent from the swarm graph.
command_surfaces.canonical_identity: severed-node entry (agent_id) over the PostgreSQL dependency graph; routing override in the live graph
command_surfaces.authority: RuVector MinCut (deterministic isolation); the cut is applied to the transient routing graph, the decision is journaled to PostgreSQL
command_surfaces.freshness: transient-derived effect (milliseconds) on the live graph; the decision journal is postgres-canonical
command_surfaces.audit_event: SHAKE256 witness event recording the sever, actor, and the weak-link cut_weight
command_surfaces.rollback: reinstate the agent (reverse routing override); the cut is graph state, reversible without data loss

command_surfaces.what_david_directs: Trigger the three-pillar ingestion of a workspace path into the database-hosted codebase.
command_surfaces.canonical_identity: new/updated codebase_table rows keyed by module_path + BLAKE3 content hash; buffered in redb, embedded, committed to PostgreSQL
command_surfaces.authority: nu_plugin (parse) -> redb (buffer) -> envctl (embed + commit) -> PostgreSQL; forward-only, PostgreSQL is the sole durable writer (STORE-001 forward-only + single-writer)
command_surfaces.freshness: buffer depth redb-live during the run; committed rows postgres-canonical after envctl flush
command_surfaces.audit_event: SHAKE256 witness event per committed block, rooted in the ingested source text hash
command_surfaces.rollback: uncommitted buffered blocks are transient (discardable); committed rows revert via PostgreSQL PITR to the pre-ingest revision
command_surfaces.filesystem_write_path: reads the workspace source; writes only redb buffer (transient) then PostgreSQL rows — no derived control-plane file

command_surfaces.what_david_directs: Project the production branch out of PostgreSQL into a clean directory for Nix/Yazelix compilation.
command_surfaces.canonical_identity: PostgreSQL production branch (branch_id) + module_path metadata -> derived filesystem tree
command_surfaces.authority: envctl projection engine, read-only from PostgreSQL (STORE-001 derived-projection class); the filesystem output is derived and disposable
command_surfaces.freshness: postgres-canonical source read; output is a deterministic snapshot of that read
command_surfaces.audit_event: SHAKE256 witness event recording the projected tree's SHA identity vs the source rows (byte-parity)
command_surfaces.rollback: delete + re-project (idempotent, deterministic); no canonical state is mutated by projection
command_surfaces.filesystem_write_path: enumerated + explicit: the ONLY sanctioned LifeOS-triggered filesystem write; derived, disposable, re-projectable — not hidden, not a control-plane detour

command_surfaces.what_david_directs: Run an ATAS ensemble simulation to forecast timelines before committing any branch.
command_surfaces.canonical_identity: simulation set (sim_id) over cloned COW staging branches; results are transient rows (never trunk truth until CS-02)
command_surfaces.authority: ATAS runtime over isolated COW staging branches (transient-derived); no durable owner until a winning branch is committed
command_surfaces.freshness: transient-derived; results readable via RS-05, discarded unless promoted
command_surfaces.audit_event: SHAKE256 witness event recording the sim_id, base branch, and actor
command_surfaces.rollback: discard all staging branches spawned by the sim (CS-03 per branch); simulation writes nothing durable

NS-CLARITY.statement: David can see and understand the whole.
NS-CLARITY.how: every visible state renders from a canonical database identity (or an explicitly-marked transient view), with lineage back to PostgreSQL

NS-DIRECTION.statement: David can direct it as one system.
NS-DIRECTION.how: every directive routes over the single control UDS into the canonical database / orchestrator with explicit authority and a witness-chain audit event

NS-PROGRESS.statement: LifeOS advances every domain without fragmenting the whole.
NS-PROGRESS.how: advancement flows forward-only (ingest, commit, materialize) through PostgreSQL as the single durable owner; no side-channel or flat-file detour can fragment the truth

invariants: No HTTP/REST: the Mission Control surface exposes no HTTP listener and issues no HTTP request; all control traffic is UDS + MessagePack (source: uds_not_http, msgpack_no_json).
No flat-file control-plane detour: no directive is spooled to a flat file and no visible state is sourced from one; redb reads are transient views, not control-plane files.
Single durable writer: LifeOS is a reader + command issuer only; every durable mutation lands in PostgreSQL via the envctl/agent writer path (STORE-001).
Forward-only sync: high-frequency directives buffer in redb and flush redb -> envctl -> PostgreSQL; no undirected redb<->PostgreSQL dual-write.
Explicit authority per surface: every visible_states[] and command_surfaces[] entry names exactly one authoritative owner.
Freshness is always labeled: every read carries its tier (redb-live | postgres-canonical | transient-derived) so a forecast or unsettled view is never mistaken for canonical truth.
Every command emits a SHAKE256 witness-chain audit event rooted in a PostgreSQL source vector; edits lacking a causal edge are rejected.
Every command has a defined rollback (COW discard, PITR, policy-version restore, or idempotent re-projection).
Every filesystem write reachable from the surface is enumerated and classified non-hidden; the sole sanctioned LifeOS-triggered write is the derived, disposable CS-07 projection.
open_dependencies: Upstream POSTGRES-003 (control-plane schema plan) and POSTGRES-006 (redb/AgentDB runtime boundary plan) are not yet materialized; canonical_identity field/table names here are the intended shapes and are refined once those plans land.

Live RuVector-in-PostgreSQL extension activation is not yet locally proven (STORE-001 open dependency); RS-03 semantic reads run over the proven Bun-loaded ruvector path until the native extension is green.
The control UDS server and redb-mmap reader are product-implementation surfaces (POSTGRES-007 defines the contract only; product execution is out of scope for this planning task).
